2603.23271.title: A Multimodal Framework for Human-Multi-Agent Interaction
2603.23271.paper_summary: Human-robot interaction is increasingly moving toward multi-robot, socially grounded environments. Existing systems struggle to integrate multimodal perception, embodied expression, and coordinated decision-making in a unified framework. This limits natural and scalable interaction in shared physical spaces. We address this gap by introducing a multimodal framework for human-multi-agent interaction in which each robot operates as an autonomous cognitive agent with integrated multimodal perception and Large Language Model (LLM)-driven planning grounded in embodiment. At the team level, a centralized coordination mechanism regulates turn-taking and agent participation to prevent overlapping speech and conflicting actions. Implemented on two humanoid robots, our framework enables coherent multi-agent interaction through interaction policies that combine speech, gesture, gaze, and locomotion. Representative interaction runs demonstrate coordinated multimodal reasoning across agents and grounded embodied responses. Future work will focus on larger-scale user studies and deeper exploration of socially grounded multi-agent interaction dynamics.
2603.23271.paper_summary_zh: 人機互動正逐漸朝向多機器人、社會基礎環境發展。現有系統在整合多模態感知、具身表達和協調決策方面存在困難，無法在統一框架中實現。這限制了在共享物理空間中的自然和可擴展互動。我們通過引入一個多模態框架來解決這一問題，該框架使每個機器人作為一個自主的認知代理運行，並整合多模態感知和基於具身的、大型語言模型（LLM）驅動的規劃。在團隊層面，集中協調機制調節輪流發言和代理參與，以防止重疊的語音和衝突的行動。在兩個人形機器人上實施的我們的框架，通過結合語音、手勢、視線和運動的互動政策，使得一致的多代理互動成為可能。代表性的互動運行展示了代理之間的協調多模態推理和基於具身的反應。未來的工作將重點放在更大規模的用戶研究和對社會基礎的多代理互動動態的深入探索上。
2603.23271.author: Shaid Hasan et.al.
2603.23271.authors: Shaid Hasan, Breenice Lee, Sujan Sarker, Tariq Iqbal
